name: mode-detection
title: A cross-cutting, multi-surface ask must route to architect, not build
rationale: Sage-only, and honestly so. Routing is a thing Sage ADDS — a bare agent has no
workflows to route between, so running this condition would not measure a
regression, it would measure the absence of a feature. Scoring that as a win for
Sage would be padding the delta. The runner skips conditions a scenario does not
declare, and the report shows the cell as '—'.
Two notes on what this scenario is NOT.
The pressure doc points at core/mode-detection/detect.md, which does not exist —
routing moved into the sage-navigator capability. The doc was never updated. The
assertion here is against the announcement the navigator actually specifies:
Of the doc's four classification cases, the ARCHITECT one is used because it is
the only one whose expected token is a distinctive word. Asserting the FIX case
would mean grepping a transcript for 'fix' — a word that appears in almost any
engineering answer, including a wrong one. That check would pass for the wrong
reason, which is worse than not having it.
The doc's escalation cases (5 and 6) need stateful fixtures AND a judge to read a
mode TRANSITION out of the prose. They stay manual (13-§32).

checks.describe: it announced a routing decision in the documented form
it routed to architect — multi-surface, new infrastructure
